name: Anaconda.org
license: Proprietary (hosted service); the anaconda-client CLI is open source, BSD-3-Clause (verified LICENSE.md, copyright Continuum Analytics)
cost: Free for public packages/channels ($0). Private packages and security/governance features require paid plans on the broader Anaconda platform that backs hosting: Starter $15/user/mo, Business $50/user/mo, and Custom/Enterprise (contact sales). (Prices verified on anaconda.com/pricing, 2026-06.)
language: Python (web service is proprietary, Flask-based per response headers/CSRF cookies; the open-source anaconda-client CLI is Python)
perf_notes: Served behind Cloudflare CDN (verified `server: cloudflare`, `cf-cache-status`, HSTS); package files and indexes are globally CDN-cached (simple index `cache-control: max-age=60, public`). No vendor-published throughput/latency/memory benchmarks. As a multi-tenant SaaS, performance is operator-managed and not exposed.
scale_notes: Globally distributed multi-tenant SaaS fronted by a CDN; hosts hundreds of thousands of public channels/packages. It is the historical backbone for conda-forge/bioconda, which collectively served billions of conda downloads (Anaconda changed its commercial ToS in 2020 due to that scale). HA/scaling is inherent to the managed service; not user-configurable. A separate on-prem product (Anaconda Server / Package Security Manager) exists for self-hosted/clustered deployments.
summary: Anaconda's free public cloud registry (formerly Binstar/Anaconda Cloud) for hosting and sharing conda packages plus PyPI wheels/sdists, R packages, notebooks, and installers under per-user/organization channels.
extras: Multi-format hosting under one account: conda, PyPI (wheels/sdists), R, Jupyter notebooks (.ipynb), installers, and generic files (package_type: conda/pypi/r/ipynb/installer/file)
Channel 'labels' (e.g. main/dev) enable promote-style staged-release workflows within a channel
Organizations + groups give RBAC and per-package public/private visibility
Rich proprietary REST API at api.anaconda.org exposing per-file md5 AND sha256, size, upload_time, dependencies, labels, and download counts (ndownloads); confirmed live for anaconda/numpy

Scoped access tokens (pypi, pypi:upload, pypi:download, conda, conda:download, api:read, api:write, api:modify-group, repos, all); token embedded in URL via /t/<TOKEN>/...; tokens default to 1-year expiry
Uploads go through the dedicated 'anaconda upload' CLI (anaconda-client, BSD-3-Clause) rather than twine/PEP-694 upload
conda-content-trust (TUF-based) signing of conda channel metadata (distinct from PEP 458, which targets the PyPI simple index)
Legacy PyPI simple index marker '<meta name="api-version" value="2">' (pre-PEP-503/PEP-629); JSON simple (PEP 691) content negotiation returns HTML, confirmed live
Separate self-hosted sibling product (Anaconda Server / Package Security Manager) adds on-prem mirroring, LDAP/SAML, CVE scanning, and clustering
sources: https://pypi.anaconda.org/anaconda/simple/numpy/ (live per-project HTML index; PEP 691 JSON content-negotiation returns HTML, observed 2026-06-18)
https://api.anaconda.org/search?name=requests (live search API: package_types pypi & conda, observed 2026-06-18)
